back: An LLM in charge of a loop: it plans, acts through tools, observes results, and repeats autonomously until a goal is reached.
Plan -> act (tool call) -> observe -> decide next step -> repeat until done.
A chatbot takes one turn and waits for you. An agent decides its own next steps across many turns - autonomy over multiple steps.
Each step builds on the last, so one wrong step becomes the foundation for everything after it. Long chains need near-perfect links.
A checkpoint where the agent proposes and a human approves - standard practice for consequential actions.

front: How to spot a real agent behind the buzzword
back: Ask: does the model decide its own next steps and act through tools, in a loop, toward a goal? If not, it is a feature, not an agent.
